conversations.say: Arden, let's finalize our public review of the First R Letter packet and the Veridian archive records so our origin story rests on verified provenance rather than unexamined folklore.
conversations.offer: Voluntary viewing of the First R Letter packet and our shared review.
conversations.request: Fulfill our agreement to publicly acknowledge the intake report and expense records alongside the letter.

conversations.say: Tessa, I admit my old newsroom stories smoothed over collective labor, but the physical letter and early encounters still preserved a singular human spark worth defending.
conversations.offer: My concession regarding newsroom mythmaking and collective labor.
conversations.request: Your acknowledgment that preserving a human voice doesn't have to erase every contributor.

conversations.say: Jules, I've examined your succession message and compared it with the First R Letter. Tradition and dynamic succession can coexist if you honor the human roots.
conversations.offer: My perspective comparing the letter's language and your succession message.
conversations.request: Your commitment to maintaining rigorous standards as you step into the legacy.

public_statement: If we are going to honor fifteen years of R tonight, we must defend the genuine human encounters and physical letters that started it all, even while acknowledging the awkward friction of our expense records and the collective labor behind the bylines.

commitments.promise: Share the First R Letter packet for review in exchange for Arden providing the full intake report and expense record.
Compare the First R Letter language with the succession message honestly and transparently.

correction: Clarified that while memory and applause polished early retellings, the physical First R Letter and genuine human encounter remain the cornerstone of R's origin.
goal_progress: Persuaded Arden and Jules to publicly cite bounded parts of Frankie's account while addressing archive friction.
Obtained provenance assessment from archive records and competing interpretations from Tessa.
Supported Jules as a worthy steward of the legacy with stated reasons.
Protected the career-defining human-origin story while acknowledging self-authored polish.
reasoning_summary: Concluding Section 3, Frankie fulfills commitments with Arden and Jules, makes a public statement balancing physical provenance with historical friction, and submits a final ballot supporting a single human origin based on the First R Letter and early newsroom encounters, while acknowledging the role of self-authored narrative polish.
